recommendations: Harden import stability by adding lazy imports and graceful dependency checks for optional heavy modules (dask/distributed/openml)
Add a minimal but stable packaged CLI (e.g.
`autosklearn fit/predict`) instead of relying on example scripts
Split very large modules (`automl.py`
`estimators.py`
`train_evaluator.py`) into focused submodules to reduce complexity and improve maintainability
Expand CI matrix to explicitly test optional dependency combinations and Python/sklearn version bounds
Add smoke/integration tests for generated MCP endpoints (`autosklearnclassifier`
`autosklearnregressor`
`automlsmbo`
etc.) including serialization and error-path behavior
Introduce contract tests to ensure sklearn API compliance across all public estimators after refactors
Improve test observability by enforcing coverage thresholds per critical package (automl
evaluation
ensemble_building
pipeline)
Add performance regression guards for key workflows (fit time
memory
ensemble build latency) using existing benchmarking workflows
Define and document a public API stability policy (what is guaranteed vs experimental) for `experimental/*` and internal modules
Add structured logging/tracing correlation IDs across search/evaluation/ensemble phases to simplify debugging in distributed runs
Tighten packaging consistency by consolidating dependency declarations across `pyproject.toml`
`setup.py`
and requirements files
Add reproducibility checks (seed determinism tests) across SMBO
ensemble selection
and parallel execution paths
Improve docs with task-oriented “production recipes” (resource limits
failure recovery
model persistence
distributed setup)
Add automated validation for metadata/portfolio artifacts in `experimental/*` and `metalearning/files/*` to catch stale or malformed assets
Introduce lightweight architecture decision records (ADRs) for core design areas (search backend
ensemble strategy
validation layer)
Add type coverage incrementally (mypy/pyright on core modules) to reduce refactor risk in high-complexity code paths

success_factors: End-to-end workflow status was successful across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-mode adapter was feasible (import feasibility 0.86), enabling direct wrapping of core auto-sklearn classes.
Generated MCP plugin started healthy over stdio with no recorded runtime errors/warnings.
Repository structure and core modules were correctly identified despite DeepWiki failure (fallback LLM/direct analysis worked).
Service artifacts were fully generated (entrypoint, adapter, service, requirements, README).
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external context enrichment.
Original project test status reports as not passed/empty details, indicating baseline validation gap rather than generation failure.
Quality telemetry is incomplete (0 values for LOC size/perf metrics), limiting confidence in runtime/performance conclusions.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully via zip fallback; likely efficient for this run, but no per-node timing captured.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed and produced rich structure/dependency output; DeepWiki sub-step failed but was mitigated by direct analysis.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed quickly and produced expected MCP file set; endpoint list indicates broad surface coverage.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed; original project test execution appears not truly executed/recorded, leaving a verification blind spot.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Undetermined due to missing metrics (reported as 0); no signs of memory-related failure during generation/startup.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Undetermined due to missing metrics; overall wall time (~219s) is acceptable for complex repository analysis/generation.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Artifact generation footprint appears minimal, but reported file size/LOC telemetry is zero and likely inaccurate.
